title: Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh
tagline: Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: The Dashavatara Temple is an early 6th century Hindu temple located at Deogarh, Lalitpur district, Uttar Pradesh which is 125 kilometers from Jhansi, in the Betwa River valley in n
overview.description: The Dashavatara Temple is an early 6th century Hindu temple located at Deogarh, Lalitpur district, Uttar Pradesh which is 125 kilometers from Jhansi, in the Betwa River valley in northern-central India. It has a simple, one cell square plan and is one of the earliest Hindu stone temples still surviving today. Built in the Gupta Period, the Dashavatara Temple at Deogarh shows the ornate Gupta style architecture. The temple at Deogarh is dedicated to Vishnu, but includes in it small footprint images of various deities such as Shiva, Parvati, Kartikeya, Brahma, Indra, the river goddesses Ganga an

topPlaces.name: Deogarh, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Deogarh is a village in Lalitpur district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on the right bank of Betwa River and to the west of Lalitpur hills.

topPlaces.name: Jain temples of Deogarh
topPlaces.description: The Jain Temple complex is group of 31 Jain temples located at Deogarh in Lalitpur district, Uttar Pradesh built around 8th to 17th century CE. The Jain complex in Deogarh are protected by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), and managed through its Northern Circle Office located in Lucknow. ASI maintain an archaeological museum at the Deogarh site, which is noted for its treasured archaeological sculptures.

topPlaces.name: Mahavir Swami Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Mahavir Swami Sanctuary, is one of many wildlife sanctuaries in Uttar Pradesh. It is 125 km from Jhansi and 33 km from Lalitpur.

items.note: Deogarh is a village in Lalitpur district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on the right bank of Betwa River and to the west of Lalitpur hills.

The Jain Temple complex is group of 31 Jain temples located at Deogarh in Lalitpur district, Uttar Pradesh built around 8th to 17th century CE. The Jain complex in Deogarh are protected by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), and managed through its Northern Circle Office located in Lucknow. ASI maintain an archaeological museum at the Deogarh site, which is noted for its treasured archaeological sculptures.
Mahavir Swami Sanctuary, is one of many wildlife sanctuaries in Uttar Pradesh. It is 125 km from Jhansi and 33 km from Lalitpur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Raja Bhoj Airport, Bhopal (166 km) and the nearest railway station is Jhansi Junction (108 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh?
faq.a: Stays in Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh?
faq.a: The top attractions are Deogarh, Uttar Pradesh, Jain temples of Deogarh, Mahavir Swami Wildlife Sanctuary.
